name: ell (LLM Programming Language)

auth.notes: OPENAI_API_KEY and other provider API keys via env vars or explicit config

performance.notes: LLM call latency; ell adds minimal overhead; versioning/tracing adds local DB writes

agent_readiness.notes: LLM programming framework treating prompts as programs. @ell.simple/@ell.complex decorators turn Python functions into LLM programs. Automatic versioning and diff tracking of prompts across runs. ELL Studio local UI for prompt inspection, versioning, and comparison. Multimodal support (images, audio). Multi-turn conversations. Works with OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, etc. via LiteLLM. Novel approach: commits make LLM programs versionable like code.

gotchas: ELL Studio adds overhead; optional but useful for development
best_when: Rapid LLM prototyping with prompt versioning and diff tracking
Teams wanting Git-like version control for prompts
Simple LLM apps where LangChain feels overengineered
Research and experimentation with prompt iteration
avoid_when: Production apps requiring mature, stable API
summary: ell treats LLM prompts as programs with @ell.simple and @ell.complex decorators. Automatic prompt versioning, diff tracking, and the ELL Studio local UI for inspecting prompt evolution across runs. Multimodal support, streaming, async. Works with OpenAI, Anthropic, and others via LiteLLM. Novel Git-like approach to prompt management. Best for rapid prototyping and teams wanting traceability — still early-stage (v0.x).
